topic.what: Making FlyClient practical for production PoW blockchain deployment via new adversary model, Zcash prover implementation, and proof size optimizations
topic.how: Introduces wa-adversary model with economic interpretation, implements FlyClient prover for Zebrad, proposes cumulative and distilled proof optimizations
topic.why_matters: Enables resource-constrained devices to verify blockchains without trusting third parties; critical for mobile wallets, IoT, and trustless cross-chain bridges

characteristics.what_it_measures: Header data size for interactive FlyClient proof under traditional c=0.5 L=100 configuration
characteristics.evidence: Paper Section IV-B: 598×1487 bytes for traditional vs 423×1487 bytes for wa-adversary at 3M blocks
characteristics.context: 3 million block Zcash chain, binary representation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proof size for non-interactive FlyClient protocol
characteristics.evidence: Paper Section IV-B: wa-adversary non-interactive proof at 504 samplings vs 802 traditional
characteristics.context: 3 million block Zcash chain, zipped representation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduction from cumulative proof optimization (no consensus change)
characteristics.evidence: Paper Section VI-C: non-interactive cumulative proof saves 9.16% vs unoptimized

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduction from distilled proof optimization (requires consensus change)
characteristics.evidence: Paper Section VI-C: distilled proof 320 KiB vs cumulative 1.09 MiB at 3M blocks
characteristics.context: Non-interactive proof, requires Ethash PoW and header distillation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Cost to pass FlyClient proof to Ethereum smart contract for trustless bridge
characteristics.evidence: Paper Section V: EIP-7623 pricing calculation at 0.125 Gwei gas price and 2100 USD ETH
characteristics.context: 1.2 MiB non-interactive zipped proof; 50,340,000 gas using EIP-7623 (40 gas/non-zero byte)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Cost reduction for distilled proofs on smart contracts
characteristics.evidence: Paper Section VI-C: 320 KiB distilled proof = 13,110,000 gas = 3.44 USD (-71.33%)
characteristics.context: Distilled proof for 3M block chain on Ethereum after Pectra hard fork

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to upgrade Zebrad database with MMR nodes for FlyClient prover
characteristics.evidence: Paper Section V: Legacy instance with old format upgrade takes ~10h for 3M blocks
characteristics.context: 3 million block Zcash chain; full sync comparable to legacy Zebrad

characteristics.what_it_measures: Impact of FlyClient prover functionality on full node sync time
characteristics.evidence: Paper Section V: MMR node generation/writing impact on total sync time is negligible; DB writes already needed for HistoryTree
characteristics.context: FlyClient-capable Zebrad syncs in comparable time to legacy instance

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of header samplings in non-interactive FlyClient proof
characteristics.vs_baseline: 802 samplings for traditional c=0.5 L=100 configuration
characteristics.evidence: Paper Section IV-B: wa-adversary parametrization yields (c*=0.2161, L*=231)
characteristics.context: 3M block chain, security parameter λ=50, adversarial block budget na=50

novelty: wa-adversary model with direct economic interpretation
First production FlyClient prover implementation for Zcash
Cumulative ancestry proof optimization without consensus change
Distilled proof optimization reducing proof size by 71%
open_problems: Integration of SNARKs with FlyClient proofs for further compression
Optimal PoW function replacement balancing ASIC-resistance and space efficiency
Scalability of prover implementation for longer chains
Formal security proofs for distilled proof optimization
